Izumo Ōka Shrine
(Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ** Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ** deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ** Deities: [[Kamōichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits. * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ** deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ** established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yū Shrine}} [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Onsen shrines]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[category:original pages]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]]

In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamōichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits. * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yū Shrine}} [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Onsen shrines]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yū Shrine | image = [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 拝殿.JPG|250px|]]
Worship hall (''haiden'') | location = 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|4.68|N|132|47|13.46|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] | shrine_rank = [[Engishiki|Shikinaisha]] (small shrine)
Former [[Kensha]] | established = Yū Shrine
(Trad.) [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō's]] reign
Izumo Ōka Shrine
(Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamōichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits. * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yū Shrine}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Onsen shrines]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]]

Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamōichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits. * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yū Shrine | image = [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 拝殿.JPG|250px|]]
Worship hall (''haiden'') | location = 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|4.68|N|132|47|13.46|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] | shrine_rank = [[Engishiki|Shikinaisha]] (small shrine)
Former [[Kensha]] | established = Yū Shrine
(Trad.) [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō's]] reign
Izumo Ōka Shrine
(Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yū Shrine}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Onsen shrines]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]]

Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yū Shrine}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Onsen shrines]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[Category:dardar]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]]

[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] | shrine_rank = [[Engishiki|Shikinaisha]] (small shrine)
Former [[Kensha]] | established = Yū Shrine
(Trad.) [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō's]] reign
Izumo Ōka Shrine
(Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yū Shrine}} [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Onsen shrines]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[Category:dardar]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]]

Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yū Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shikinaisha]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Prefectural shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Onsen shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]]

The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yū Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shikinaisha]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Prefectural shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Onsen shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]]

[[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yū Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shikinaisha]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Prefectural shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Onsen shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]]

Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yū Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shikinaisha]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Prefectural shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Onsen shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTYu Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTYu Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTYu Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTYu Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTYu Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yū Shrine | image = [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 拝殿.JPG|250px|]]
Worship hall (''haiden'') | location = 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|4.68|N|132|47|13.46|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] | shrine_rank = [[Engishiki|Shikinaisha]] (small shrine)
Former [[Kensha]] | established = Yū Shrine
(Trad.) [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō's]] reign
Izumo Ōka Shrine
(Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT::Yū Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT::Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shikinaisha]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Prefectural shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Onsen shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]]

Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT::Yū Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT::Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shikinaisha]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Prefectural shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Onsen shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]]

Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Yū Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shikinaisha]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Prefectural shrines]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Onsen shrines]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yu Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yū Shrine | image = [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 拝殿.JPG|250px|]]
Worship hall (''haiden'') | location = 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|4.68|N|132|47|13.46|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] | shrine_rank = [[Engishiki|Shikinaisha]] (small shrine)
Former [[Kensha]] | established = Yū Shrine
(Trad.) [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō's]] reign
Izumo Ōka Shrine
(Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Yū Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shikinaisha]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Prefectural shrines]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Onsen shrines]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yu Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]]

[[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yū Shrine | image = [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 拝殿.JPG|250px|]]
Worship hall (''haiden'') | location = 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|4.68|N|132|47|13.46|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]

(Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}}
