{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yū Shrine | image = [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 拝殿.JPG|250px|]]
Worship hall (''haiden'') | location = 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|4.68|N|132|47|13.46|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] | shrine_rank = [[Engishiki|Shikinaisha]] (small shrine)
Former [[Kensha]] | established = Yū Shrine
(Trad.) [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō's]] reign
Izumo Ōka Shrine
(Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' (Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yū Shrine | image = [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 拝殿.JPG|250px|]]
Worship hall (''haiden'') | location = 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|4.68|N|132|47|13.46|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] | shrine_rank = [[Engishiki|Shikinaisha]] (small shrine)
Former [[Kensha]] | established = Yū Shrine
(Trad.) [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō's]] reign
Izumo Ōka Shrine
(Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Yū Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Shikinaisha]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Prefectural shrines]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Onsen shrines]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}}[[Category:Ugoodbye $1 categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yū Shrine | image = [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 拝殿.JPG|250px|]]
Worship hall (''haiden'') | location = 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|4.68|N|132|47|13.46|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] | shrine_rank = [[Engishiki|Shikinaisha]] (small shrine)
Former [[Kensha]] | established = Yū Shrine
(Trad.) [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō's]] reign
Izumo Ōka Shrine
(Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yū Shrine | image = [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 拝殿.JPG|250px|]]
Worship hall (''haiden'') | location = 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|4.68|N|132|47|13.46|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] | shrine_rank = [[Engishiki|Shikinaisha]] (small shrine)
Former [[Kensha]] | established = Yū Shrine
(Trad.) [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō's]] reign
Izumo Ōka Shrine
(Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yū Shrine | image = [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 拝殿.JPG|250px|]]
Worship hall (''haiden'') | location = 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|4.68|N|132|47|13.46|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] | shrine_rank = [[Engishiki|Shikinaisha]] (small shrine)
Former [[Kensha]] | established = Yū Shrine
(Trad.) [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō's]] reign
Izumo Ōka Shrine
(Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Yū Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yū Shrine | image = [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 拝殿.JPG|250px|]]
Worship hall (''haiden'') | location = 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|4.68|N|132|47|13.46|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] | shrine_rank = [[Engishiki|Shikinaisha]] (small shrine)
Former [[Kensha]] | established = Yū Shrine
(Trad.) [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō's]] reign
Izumo Ōka Shrine
(Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Yū Shrine}} (Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} [[ja:湯神社]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Yū Shrine}} In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yū Shrine | image = [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 拝殿.JPG|250px|]]
Worship hall (''haiden'') | location = 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|4.68|N|132|47|13.46|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] | shrine_rank = [[Engishiki|Shikinaisha]] (small shrine)
Former [[Kensha]] | established = Yū Shrine
(Trad.) [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō's]] reign
Izumo Ōka Shrine
(Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Yū Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yu Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:11-01-18]] [[Category:111-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:湯神社]] [[sn:Yu Shrine]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}

Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁|12=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Yū Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yu Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:111-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:湯神社]] [[sn:Yu Shrine]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}

Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Yū Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:湯神社]] [[sn:Yu Shrine]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}}

It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' revision.comment: 31 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:湯神社 up to rev 97425145
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 湯神社 |画像 = [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 拝殿.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[愛媛県]][[松山市]]道後湯之町4-10 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|51|4.68|N|132|47|13.46|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title|name=湯神社}} |祭神 = [[大国主|大己貴命]]
[[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[県社]] |創建 = 湯神社
（伝）[[景行天皇]]年間
出雲崗神社
（伝）[[孝霊天皇]]年間 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 湯月大明神・四社大明神・西宮 |例祭 = [[10月6日]] |神事 = 初子祭（1月第2日曜）
湯祈祷祭（[[3月19日]]-[[3月21日|21日]]） }} '''湯神社'''（ゆじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]][[冠山 (松山市)|冠山]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。神紋は「亀甲に花菱」。 相殿に式内社の'''出雲崗神社'''（いずものおかじんじゃ）を祀る。別称として、「湯月大明神」・4柱の神を祀るため「四社大明神」・[[伊佐爾波神社]]の西にあるため「西宮」とも呼ばれる。 == 概要 == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|入り口の[[注連縄]]]] [[松山市]]市街地の東部、[[道後温泉]]近くの[[冠山 (松山市)|冠山]]山頂に鎮座する。 社名は道後温泉によるもので、古くは道後温泉の起源地である鷺谷（現 松山市道後鷺谷町）に鎮座していた。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * 大己貴命 （おおなむちのみこと） - [[大国主|大国主命]]の異称 * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] （すくなびこなのみこと） :: 『伊予国風土記』逸文には、この2神の[[道後温泉]]に関する伝承が記載されている（「[[道後温泉#歴史]]」を参照）。 ; 相殿神（出雲崗神社祭神） * [[スサノオ|素盞嗚命]] （すさのおのみこと） * [[クシナダヒメ|稻田姫命]] （くしなだひめのみこと） == 歴史 == 湯神社は、[[景行天皇]]が皇后・八坂入姫命とともに当地に行幸した際、[[道後温泉]]の守護神として、鷺谷の大禅寺の前に創建されたと伝えられる。鷺谷は、鷺が痛めた足を温泉に浸して治療していたとされる場所で、[[道後温泉]]発見の起源地である。また、鷺は道後温泉のシンボルにもなっている。[[舒明天皇]]の[[行幸]]に際し、社殿が新築された。 また、現社地の冠山（出雲崗）には出雲崗神社が鎮座していた。[[孝霊天皇]]の創建で、素盞嗚尊・稲田姫命・[[オオヤマツミ|大山積命]]・[[カヤノヒメ|茅野姫命]]の四柱の神を祀り、四所大明神とも称していた。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』には、湯神社は「[[伊予国]][[温泉郡]] 湯神社」、出雲崗神社は「伊予国温泉郡 出雲崗神社」として記載され、ともに[[式内社]]に列している。 [[大永]]年間、地震で温泉が埋没し、湯神社の社殿も大破した。そのため、[[河野通直 (弾正少弼)|河野通直]]により湯神社は出雲崗神社に[[合祀]]され、湯月大明神、四社大明神などと称された。 [[元禄]]年間、社号を湯神社・出雲崗神社に復した。 宝永4年（[[1707年]]）、[[宝永地震|地震]]により出湯が停止した際、湯神社で出湯の祈祷が行われ、再び現在のように温泉が出たという。宝永5年（[[1708年]]）、湯神社はその功により、相殿から境内別社となった。 明治4年（[[1871年]]）、湯神社に出雲崗神社を合祀する現在の形式となった。参道前の社号碑は、[[出雲大社]]の第82代[[出雲国造]][[千家尊統]]の書である（伊佐爾波神社も同じ）。
湯神社・出雲崗神社 経緯
{{familytree/start|style=font-size:90%}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left| 01|02|-|03|.| 01='''［鷺谷］'''|02=湯神社|03=（地震で大破）}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left| 01|02|-|-|-|-|^|03|-|04|-|05| 01='''［[[冠山 (松山市)|冠山]]（現在地）］'''|02=出雲崗神社|03=出雲崗神社
（相殿に湯神社）|04=出雲崗神社・湯神社
（境内別社）|05=湯神社
（相殿に出雲崗神社）}} {{familytree/end}}
== 摂末社 == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|中嶋神社]] * 三穂社 ** 祭神：[[事代主|事代主命]]（ことしろぬしのみこと）、[[ヒルコ|蛭兒命]]（ひるこのみこと） * 八幡若宮社 ** 祭神：大鷦鷯尊（おおさざきのみこと、[[仁徳天皇]]） * 児守社 ** 祭神：[[神大市比売|神大市姫命]]（かむおおいちひめ）、[[鎭疫神]]、[[河野通広]] ** 古くは[[松山城 (伊予国)|松山城]]東麓にあったが、松山城築城の際に湯神社の末社・河野霊神に合祀された。河野霊神は[[時宗]]の開祖[[一遍]] が父・河野通広を祀るために建立したもの。遊行上人が巡教した際には必ず参拝を行い、その都度領主は社殿を造営整備したとされる。鎮疫神は、安政6年に悪病流行につき鎮疫のために勧請された。 * 中嶋神社 ** 祭神：[[田道間守|田道間守命]]（たじまもりのみこと） ** 昭和32年（[[1957年]]）3月、[[四国]]四県の製菓業者によって、[[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]にある、製菓・柑橘の祖神である[[中嶋神社]]の御分霊を迎えて、四国分社として創建された。 == 主な祭事 ==
湯神社 年間祭事一覧
* 1月 ** [[歳旦祭]] （[[1月1日]]） ** [[元始祭]] （[[1月3日]]） ** 初子祭 （1月第2日曜） ::: 以前は旧暦11月の子の日に行われていた。祭当日は参拝者で大変賑わう。大己貴命が[[鼠]]に救われたという[[古事記]]の伝説から、鼠を[[十二支]]の[[子 (十二支)|子]]に結びつけた事に基づき、社殿建立の日、修理の始まる日なども、すべてこの由緒によって子の日を選んで施行されている。 * 3月 ** 湯祈祷祭 （[[3月19日]]-[[3月21日|21日]]） ::: 期間中には、松山春まつり（道後温泉まつり）が開催されている。 * 5月 ** 中嶋神社春祭 （[[5月19日]]） * 10月 ** 宵宮 （[[10月5日]]） ** [[例大祭]] （[[10月6日]]） ** [[神幸祭]] （[[10月7日]]） ::: [[喧嘩神輿]]（[[鉢合わせ]]）が行われる。 ::: 道後地区の鉢合わせには、湯神社から、「小唐人（ことうじん）」、「北小唐人（きたことうじん）」、伊佐爾波神社（いさにわじんじゃ）から、「湯之町（ゆのまち）」、「道後（どうご）」、「築山（つきやま）」、「溝辺（みぞのべ）」、「大唐人（おおとうじん）」、「持田（もちだ）」の八町八体の[[神輿]]が参加する。 * 11月 ** 綿着・[[七五三]]祭 （[[11月15日]]） ** 中嶋神社秋祭 （[[11月19日]]） * 12月 ** [[天長祭]] （[[12月23日]]）
== 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[愛媛県]][[松山市]]道後湯之町4-10 ; 交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[伊予鉄道]][[伊予鉄道城南線|城南線]]（市内電車） [[道後温泉駅]] （徒歩4分） ; 周辺 * [[道後温泉]] ** [[道後温泉本館]] ** [[椿の湯]] ** 放生園 - からくり時計・足湯などのある小公園 ** [[道後商店街]] * [[道後公園]]（[[湯築城]]跡） * [[松山市立子規記念博物館]] * [[伊佐爾波神社]] * [[松山神社 (松山市)|松山神社]] * [[石手寺]] - [[四国霊場]]第五十一番札所 * [[宝厳寺 (松山市)|宝厳寺]] - [[時宗]]開祖の[[一遍]]上人誕生地 * [[常信寺 (松山市)|常信寺]] == 参考文献 == * 『日本歴史地名大系 愛媛県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）松山市 湯神社項 == 外部リンク == *[https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ 湯神社]（公式サイト） * [http://ehime-jinjacyo.jp/?p=887 湯神社]（愛媛県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ゆしんしや}} [[Category:松山市の神社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:温泉神社]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 湯神社 |画像 = [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 拝殿.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[愛媛県]][[松山市]]道後湯之町4-10 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|51|4.68|N|132|47|13.46|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title|name=湯神社}} |祭神 = [[大国主|大己貴命]]
[[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[県社]] |創建 = 湯神社
（伝）[[景行天皇]]年間
出雲崗神社
（伝）[[孝霊天皇]]年間 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 湯月大明神・四社大明神・西宮 |例祭 = [[10月6日]] |神事 = 初子祭（1月第2日曜）
湯祈祷祭（[[3月19日]]-[[3月21日|21日]]） }} '''湯神社'''（ゆじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]][[冠山 (松山市)|冠山]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。神紋は「亀甲に花菱」。 相殿に式内社の'''出雲崗神社'''（いずものおかじんじゃ）を祀る。別称として、「湯月大明神」・4柱の神を祀るため「四社大明神」・[[伊佐爾波神社]]の西にあるため「西宮」とも呼ばれる。 == 概要 == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|入り口の[[注連縄]]]] [[松山市]]市街地の東部、[[道後温泉]]近くの[[冠山 (松山市)|冠山]]山頂に鎮座する。 社名は道後温泉によるもので、古くは道後温泉の起源地である鷺谷（現 松山市道後鷺谷町）に鎮座していた。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * 大己貴命 （おおなむちのみこと） - [[大国主|大国主命]]の異称 * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] （すくなびこなのみこと） :: 『伊予国風土記』逸文には、この2神の[[道後温泉]]に関する伝承が記載されている（「[[道後温泉#歴史]]」を参照）。 ; 相殿神（出雲崗神社祭神） * [[スサノオ|素盞嗚命]] （すさのおのみこと） * [[クシナダヒメ|稻田姫命]] （くしなだひめのみこと） == 歴史 == 湯神社は、[[景行天皇]]が皇后・八坂入姫命とともに当地に行幸した際、[[道後温泉]]の守護神として、鷺谷の大禅寺の前に創建されたと伝えられる。鷺谷は、鷺が痛めた足を温泉に浸して治療していたとされる場所で、[[道後温泉]]発見の起源地である。また、鷺は道後温泉のシンボルにもなっている。[[舒明天皇]]の[[行幸]]に際し、社殿が新築された。 また、現社地の冠山（出雲崗）には出雲崗神社が鎮座していた。[[孝霊天皇]]の創建で、素盞嗚尊・稲田姫命・[[オオヤマツミ|大山積命]]・[[カヤノヒメ|茅野姫命]]の四柱の神を祀り、四所大明神とも称していた。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』には、湯神社は「[[伊予国]][[温泉郡]] 湯神社」、出雲崗神社は「伊予国温泉郡 出雲崗神社」として記載され、ともに[[式内社]]に列している。 [[大永]]年間、地震で温泉が埋没し、湯神社の社殿も大破した。そのため、[[河野通直 (弾正少弼)|河野通直]]により湯神社は出雲崗神社に[[合祀]]され、湯月大明神、四社大明神などと称された。 [[元禄]]年間、社号を湯神社・出雲崗神社に復した。 宝永4年（[[1707年]]）、[[宝永地震|地震]]により出湯が停止した際、湯神社で出湯の祈祷が行われ、再び現在のように温泉が出たという。宝永5年（[[1708年]]）、湯神社はその功により、相殿から境内別社となった。 明治4年（[[1871年]]）、湯神社に出雲崗神社を合祀する現在の形式となった。参道前の社号碑は、[[出雲大社]]の第82代[[出雲国造]][[千家尊統]]の書である（伊佐爾波神社も同じ）。
湯神社・出雲崗神社 経緯

（相殿に湯神社）|04=出雲崗神社・湯神社
（境内別社）|05=湯神社
（相殿に出雲崗神社）}} {{familytree/end}}

* 1月 ** [[歳旦祭]] （[[1月1日]]） ** [[元始祭]] （[[1月3日]]） ** 初子祭 （1月第2日曜） ::: 以前は旧暦11月の子の日に行われていた。祭当日は参拝者で大変賑わう。大己貴命が[[鼠]]に救われたという[[古事記]]の伝説から、鼠を[[十二支]]の[[子 (十二支)|子]]に結びつけた事に基づき、社殿建立の日、修理の始まる日なども、すべてこの由緒によって子の日を選んで施行されている。 * 3月 ** 湯祈祷祭 （[[3月19日]]-[[3月21日|21日]]） ::: 期間中には、松山春まつり（道後温泉まつり）が開催されている。 * 5月 ** 中嶋神社春祭 （[[5月19日]]） * 10月 ** 宵宮 （[[10月5日]]） ** [[例大祭]] （[[10月6日]]） ** [[神幸祭]] （[[10月7日]]） ::: [[喧嘩神輿]]（[[鉢合わせ]]）が行われる。 ::: 道後地区の鉢合わせには、湯神社から、「小唐人（ことうじん）」、「北小唐人（きたことうじん）」、伊佐爾波神社（いさにわじんじゃ）から、「湯之町（ゆのまち）」、「道後（どうご）」、「築山（つきやま）」、「溝辺（みぞのべ）」、「大唐人（おおとうじん）」、「持田（もちだ）」の八町八体の[[神輿]]が参加する。 * 11月 ** 綿着・[[七五三]]祭 （[[11月15日]]） ** 中嶋神社秋祭 （[[11月19日]]） * 12月 ** [[天長祭]] （[[12月23日]]）
