{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yū Shrine | image = [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 拝殿.JPG|250px|]]
Worship hall (''haiden'') | location = 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|4.68|N|132|47|13.46|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]] | shrine_Rank = [[Engishiki|Shikinaisha]] (small shrine)
Former [[Kensha]] | established = Yū Shrine
(Trad.) [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō's]] reign
Izumo Ōka Shrine
(Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Yū Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{citation needed|Yu Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:湯神社]] [[sn:Yu Shrine]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yu Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|湯神社|version=97425145|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' (Trad.) [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō's]] reign
Izumo Ōka Shrine
(Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunahikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Shrine Association|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|湯神社|version=97425145|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:湯神社]] [[sn:Yu Shrine]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]]

In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' (Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Shrine Association|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|湯神社|version=97425145|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:湯神社]] [[sn:Yu Shrine]] [[en:draft:Yu Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]]

In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' (Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' (Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[:en:1957|1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[:en:1957|1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Izumo Ōka Shrine
(Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[Hiruko|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[:en:1957|1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Shrine Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|湯神社|version=97425145|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:湯神社]] [[sn:Yu Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] {{Commons category|Yu-jinja (Matsuyama)}}

In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[:en:1957|1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Shrine Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|湯神社|version=97425145|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:湯神社]] [[sn:Yu Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] {{Commons category|Yu-jinja (Matsuyama)}}

Izumo Ōka Shrine
(Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[:en:1957|1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[:en:Ishite-ji|Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Shrine Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|湯神社|version=97425145|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:湯神社]] [[sn:Yu Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] {{Commons category|Yu-jinja (Matsuyama)}}

During the [[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[:en:1957|1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[:en:Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[:en:Ishite-ji|Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[:en:1957|1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[:en:Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[:en:Ishite-ji|Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[:en:1957|1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[:en:Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[:en:Ishite-ji|Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Shrine Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|湯神社|version=97425145|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:湯神社]] [[sn:Yu Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] {{Commons category|Yu-jinja (Matsuyama)}}

[[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[:en:1957|1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[:en:Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[:en:Ishite-ji|Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11563773]] Wikidata name: Yu Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yū Shrine | image = File:湯神社 (松山市) 拝殿.JPG | location = 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33.8513|N|132.787072|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}} | shrine_Rank = [[Engishiki|Shikinaisha]] (small shrine)
Former [[Kensha]] | established = Yū Shrine
(Trad.) [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō's]] reign
Izumo Ōka Shrine
(Trad.) [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime | prefecture= {{ill|Dōgo Yunomachi|qid=Q11641717}} | founded_by= {{ill|Keikō|qid=Q329723}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[:en:1957|1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[:en:Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[:en:Ishite-ji|Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Shrine Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|湯神社|version=97425145|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:湯神社]] [[sn:Yu Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] {{Commons category|Yu-jinja (Matsuyama)}}

The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[:en:1957|1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[:en:Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[:en:Ishite-ji|Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Shrine Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|湯神社|version=97425145|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:湯神社]] [[sn:Yu Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]][[sn:Yu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] {{Commons category|Yu-jinja (Matsuyama)}}

In [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[:en:1957|1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[:en:Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[:en:Ishite-ji|Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign | map_type = Japan Ehime | prefecture= {{ill|Dōgo Yunomachi|qid=Q11641717}} | founded_by= {{ill|Keikō|qid=Q329723}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Yū Shrine''' (湯神社, ''Yū Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of [[Mount Kanjō]] in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[:en:1957|1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[:en:Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[:en:Ishite-ji|Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Shrine Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|湯神社|version=97425145|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:湯神社]] [[sn:Yu Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] [[sn:Yu Shrine]][[sn:Shikinaisha]][[sn:Yu Shrine]][[sn:Shikinaisha]] {{Commons category|Yu-jinja (Matsuyama)}}

It is an [[Engishiki|Engishiki-listed shrine]] and a former [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Its crest is the "hexagonal flower crest" (''kikkō ni hanabishi''). The shrine shares its grounds with the '''Izumo Ōka Shrine''' (出雲崗神社, ''Izumo no Oka Jinja''), enshrined as an auxiliary deity. Known by other names, such as "Yutsuki Daimyōjin," "Shisha Daimyōjin" (Shrine of Four Gods), and "Nishinomiya" (Western Shrine) due to its location west of [[Isaniwa Shrine]]. == Overview == [[File:湯神社 (松山市) 注連縄.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entrance shimenawa]] Yū Shrine is situated in the eastern part of [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], close to the famous [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]. The shrine's name derives from the nearby Dōgo Onsen hot springs. Historically, it was located at the hot spring's origin point in [[Sagi Valley]], a site linked to the discovery of the springs. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deities * '''[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]''' (大己貴命) – An alternate name for Ōkuninushi. * '''[[Sukunabikona|Sukunahikona no Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) – Associated with the origin myths of [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]] (see [[Dōgo Onsen#History]]). ; Auxiliary deities (Izumo Ōka Shrine) * '''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚命) * '''[[Kushinadahime|Kushiinadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) == History == According to tradition, Yū Shrine was established during the reign of [[Keikō (emperor)|Emperor Keikō]] when he visited the area with Empress [[Yasakairihime]]. The shrine was originally located in [[Sagi Valley]], near the hot springs believed to have been discovered when a heron (''sagi'') healed its injured leg in the waters. This heron remains a symbol of Dōgo Onsen. Izumo Ōka Shrine, located at the current site on Mount Kanjō, was said to have been founded during [[Kōrei (emperor)|Emperor Kōrei's]] reign. It enshrines four deities, earning it the name "Shisha Daimyōjin." Both shrines are listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records: Yū Shrine as "Yū Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province," and Izumo Ōka Shrine as "Izumo Ōka Shrine of Onsen District, Iyo Province." During the [[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei]] era, an earthquake caused significant damage to the springs and Yū Shrine. It was subsequently merged with Izumo Ōka Shrine under the name Yutsuki Daimyōjin. Later, during the [[Genroku (era)|Genroku]] period, the shrines regained their separate identities. In [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] (1871), the two shrines were unified under Yū Shrine's name, with Izumo Ōka Shrine enshrined as an auxiliary deity. == Subordinate Shrines == [[File:中嶋神社 (湯神社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Nakajima Shrine]] * '''Mihosha''' ''' Deities: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] (事代主命), [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Hiruko no Mikoto]] (蛭児命) * '''Hachiman Wakamiya Shrine''' ''' deity: Ōsazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯尊, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]) * '''Kosori Shrine''' ''' Deities: [[Kamuo Ichihime|Kamōichi-hime no Mikoto]] (神大市姫命), other local spirits.''' * '''Nakajima Shrine''' ''' deity: [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori no Mikoto]] (田道間守命) ''' established in [[:en:1957|1957]] as the Shikoku branch of [[Nakajima Shrine]] in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. == festivals == * January: ''Hatsuko festival'' – Celebrates the Year of the Rat, linking mythology to the shrine's deities. * March: ''Yukito festival'' – Held concurrently with the Matsuyama Spring festival. * October: ''Reitaisai'' – Features ''kenka mikoshi'' (clashing portable shrines) from local districts. == Access == * '''location''': 4-10 Dōgo Yunochō, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. * Nearest station: [[:en:Iyo Railway|Iyo Railways]] Dōgo Onsen Station (4-minute walk). == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dōgo Onsen]]: [[Dōgo Onsen Honkan]], Tsubaki-no-Yu, and surrounding attractions. * [[Isaniwa Shrine]] * [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku Pilgrimage]] temples, including [[:en:Ishite-ji|Ishite-ji]] (51st temple). == References == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ehime-ken no Chimei.'' Heibonsha. == External Links == * [https://yu-jinja.official.jp/ Yū Shrine Official Website] * {{ill|Ehime Shrine Association|ja|愛媛県神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Shrine Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|湯神社|version=97425145|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:湯神社]] [[sn:Yu Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Yu Shrine]][[sn:Shikinaisha]][[sn:Yu Shrine]][[sn:Shikinaisha]] {{Commons category|Yu-jinja (Matsuyama)}}
